content.stem: Which group does NOT follow the same rule? Group A: cat, dog, rabbit Group B: car, bus, truck Group C: apple, banana, orange Group D: happy, sad, book
content.explanation: Groups A, B, and C each contain three items from the same category (animals, vehicles, fruits). Group D mixes two emotions with one object, so it doesn't follow the same rule.

metadata.misconception_targeted: focusing on surface features rather than categorical consistency

distractor_rationale.reasoning: Student might think Group A doesn't belong because it contains living things while others seem non-living
Student might focus on vehicles being machines rather than seeing the categorical consistency
Student might think fruits don't belong because they're natural while other groups seem artificial
